REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT''S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER TO THE THIRD (SLANDER OF TITLE) AND FOURTH (UCL VIOLATION) CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF CONTRACT) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO AUTHORITIES CITED BY PLAINTIFF FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO BENEFICIARY/LENDER IS NOT ENTITLED TO SUBSTITUTE THE TRUSTEE PER THE TERMS OF THE DEED OF TRUST. DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION (IIED) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT PLED AND NO POSSIBILITY OF AMENDMENT SUGGESTED. DEMURER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HBOR VIOLATION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IN LIGHT OF CC 2923.6(C); CC 2923.6(G); WITHERS V. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK 2014 WL 3418367; WILLIAMS V. WELLS FARGO BANK 2014 WL 1568857. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Tamer(818-784-1400) of Tamer Law Group for Plaintiff/opposing party Mark Mendaros through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

Plaintiff did not comply with the tentative ruling rule. Plaintiff requests 30 days to amend the complaint. The Court grants the request as long as other side does not oppose. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant''s Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is Sustained. Demurrer to the third (slander of title) and fourth (UCL violation) causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. Demurrer to the second cause of action (breach of contract) is sustained without leave to amend. No authorities cited by Plaintiff for the proposition that the successor in interest to beneficiary/lender is not entitled to substitute the Trustee per the terms of the Deed of Trust. Demurrer to the fifth cause of action (IIED) is sustained without leave to amend. No outrageous conduct pled and no possibility of amendment suggested. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for HBOR violation for Plaintiff to allege all elements of this cause of action in light of CC 2923.6(c); CC 2923.6(g); Withers v. JP Morgan Chase Bank 2014 WL 3418367; Williams v. Wells Fargo Bank 2014 WL 1568857. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
